_note: Phase 2 additions for the reasoning/ area. Sandbox/curl was denied during this pass, so primary PDFs for newly-added papers were NOT downloaded; their `local_file` is null, and `excerpts_file` points to a skeleton with verbatim abstracts but body-section quotes pending follow-up. The deepseek-r1 and snell2024 excerpts were populated by parallel subagents during Phase 2 and are full-quality.

new_papers.title: Large Language Models are Zero-Shot Reasoners
new_papers.summary: Zero-shot CoT: appending 'Let's think step by step' elicits CoT-style reasoning without exemplars. text-davinci-002 MultiArith 17.7% -> 78.7%; GSM8K 10.4% -> 40.7%. Two-stage prompt: reasoning extraction + answer extraction.

new_papers.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
new_papers.authors: Wang, Wei, Schuurmans, Le, Chi, Narang, Chowdhery, Zhou
new_papers.summary: Self-consistency: sample N reasoning paths at T > 0, majority-vote over extracted answers. GSM8K +17.9%, SVAMP +11.0%, AQuA +12.2% over greedy CoT. The cheapest test-time-compute strategy and the baseline against which best-of-N + verifier methods are compared.

new_papers.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
new_papers.authors: Lightman, Kosaraju, Burda, Edwards, Baker, Lee, Leike, Schulman, Sutskever, Cobbe
new_papers.summary: Process Reward Models on PRM800K. Process supervision substantially outperforms outcome supervision on MATH best-of-N at matched N. min-aggregation over per-step PRM scores is the strongest aggregator. Released the PRM800K dataset (800K human step-labels).
